{"key_event":"Republican consolidation amid Dreyfus-era mass mobilization","reasoning":"The Republic’s schools, conscription, prefectural administration, policing powers, and colonial-military apparatus deepened state capacity, though Panama, anarchist violence, Fashoda, and Dreyfus damaged legitimacy. Civil society also grew stronger through the popular press, labor organization, socialism, leagues, strikes, and Dreyfusard/anti-Dreyfusard campaigns, despite the repressive anti-anarchist laws.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":8.4,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":8.5}
